supports the PD per device addressing model which is an addressing model which is will soon be published by the ITF as RFC it's in the RFC editor que and so this is sort of kind of a baked model that the ITF has documented and just like we have the a flag for slack and the m flag for dpv 6 we have a flag that signals this why do we need a flag that the most important reason we need a flag is that if a device implements this model on the device side and it turns it on it will ask for a prefix that is slack sized because that's what the P PD per device draft says and on many networks such as home networks it will get one and that'll all work as intended the problem is if there are too many devices that ask for these prefixes and get them the network will run out of space if you have a sl60 at home you've got 16 devices 16 phones ask for prefixes your network is now out of space right so this is why the device needs this is why a flag is needed there are other reasons why it might be nice to have a flag like say oh try PD or don't try it but that those are secondary this is the reason why we need it and again why slack size prefix because that's what the model sets the model that we have published as an RFC or will be right uh okay next slide um there's been a um after last call there's been a sort of Fairly uh Lively discussion that as I said that the operational model networks right this is one of the problems we're trying to solve here home networks you ask for 64 because that's what PD device per device says you get it and then you burn down the network right so
